title: [InstCombine] Fold icmp (vreduce_(or|and) %x), (0|-1)
author: artagnon
description:
We can just compare the entire bitcasted value against Zero or AllOnes in this case.

It is profitable on all major targets: https://godbolt.org/z/o7ecKjbsK

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
I think we're seeing regressions on llvm-test-suite with -march=rva23u64 -O3 with this patch unfortunately.

```diff
--- build.rva23u64-O3-a/MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/CMakeFiles/nbench.dir/emfloat.s   2026-02-23 18:31:33
+++ build.rva23u64-O3-b//MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/CMakeFiles/nbench.dir/emfloat.s  2026-02-23 18:26:08
@@ -1796,11 +1832,16 @@
        .cfi_offset s0, -16
        .cfi_offset s1, -24
        addi    s0, a0, 4
-       vsetivli        zero, 4, e16, mf2, ta, ma
-       vle16.v v8, (s0)
-       vredor.vs       v8, v8, v8
-       vmv.x.s a2, v8
-       slli    a2, a2, 48
+       lhu     a2, 2(s0)
+       lhu     a3, 0(s0)
+       lhu     a4, 4(s0)
+       lhu     a5, 6(s0)
+       slli    a2, a2, 16
+       or      a2, a2, a3
+       slli    a4, a4, 32
+       slli    a5, a5, 48
+       or      a4, a4, a5
+       or      a2, a2, a4
        beqz    a2, .LBB4_5
```

We remove the domain crossing but we go from 5 instructions to 10. AFAIK all of the vector instructions are single uops at this LMUL. 

For RISC-V the single vector reduction is cheaper than the series of binary ops I think, and the scalarization inadvertently causes multiple scalar loads because of alignment I'm guessing. Would it be possible to look at doing this in VectorCombine instead?

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> I think we're seeing regressions on llvm-test-suite with -march=rva23u64 -O3 with this patch unfortunately.
> 
> ```diff
> --- build.rva23u64-O3-a/MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/CMakeFiles/nbench.dir/emfloat.s   2026-02-23 18:31:33
> +++ build.rva23u64-O3-b//MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/CMakeFiles/nbench.dir/emfloat.s  2026-02-23 18:26:08
> @@ -1796,11 +1832,16 @@
>         .cfi_offset s0, -16
>         .cfi_offset s1, -24
>         addi    s0, a0, 4
> -       vsetivli        zero, 4, e16, mf2, ta, ma
> -       vle16.v v8, (s0)
> -       vredor.vs       v8, v8, v8
> -       vmv.x.s a2, v8
> -       slli    a2, a2, 48
> +       lhu     a2, 2(s0)
> +       lhu     a3, 0(s0)
> +       lhu     a4, 4(s0)
> +       lhu     a5, 6(s0)
> +       slli    a2, a2, 16
> +       or      a2, a2, a3
> +       slli    a4, a4, 32
> +       slli    a5, a5, 48
> +       or      a4, a4, a5
> +       or      a2, a2, a4
>         beqz    a2, .LBB4_5
> ```

Strange, I wonder where all the extra operations are coming from? Don't we simply fold it into an icmp bitcast(%vec), (AllOnes|Zero), removing the or altogether? Let's try to make sure we have the correct diff? I will also run it on my end.

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body: Ah yes, we're probably missing a one-use constraint :/

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
> Ah yes, we're probably missing a one-use constraint :/

I'm not sure if this is related to a one-use constraint. The reduction in that code snippet only has one use, it just happens to be lowered to `vredror.vs v8, v8, v8`. I think the IR for it is something like

```llvm
%v = load <4 x i16>
%red = call i16 @llvm.vector.reduce.or(<4 x i16> %v)
%cmp = icmp eq i16 %red, ...
```

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> I'm not sure if this is related to a one-use constraint.

Hm, my reading of the snippet is that we're branching on `a2` (so that would be the icmp operand, which matches the intrinsic vredor + vmx.x.s), and it has the `slli` use? What am I missing?

> I think the IR for it is something like

I checked this already (https://godbolt.org/z/Mh9zx43es), and the InstCombine patch should  be doing the right thing in for the middle-end with one-use constraint?

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
> > I'm not sure if this is related to a one-use constraint.
> 
> Hm, my reading of the snippet is that we're branching on `a2` (so that would be the icmp operand, which matches the intrinsic vredor + vmx.x.s), and it has the `slli` use? What am I missing?


The slli writes to a2 so it's still one use, the original codegen is:

```asm
       vsetivli        zero, 4, e16, mf2, ta, ma
       vle16.v v8, (s0)
       vredor.vs       v8, v8, v8
       vmv.x.s a2, v8
       slli    a2, a2, 48
       beqz    a2, .LBB4_5
```

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> The slli writes to a2 so it's still one use

Ah yes, but there must be something else going on in DAGCombine or something -- I just codegen'ed emfloat with the one-use patch, and I don't see the snippet in the diff: do you have some script to filter the diff for regressions or something? Can you kindly verify on your end?

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
I finally found it, but the diff is inverted for me!

```diff
-       .loc    1 256 12                        # MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.
c:256:12 @[ MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.c:441:5 @[ MultiSource/Benchmarks/nben
ch/emfloat.c:482:9 ] ]
-       lhu     a2, 2(s0)
-       lhu     a3, 0(s0)
-       lhu     a4, 4(s0)
-       lhu     a5, 6(s0)
-       slli    a2, a2, 16
-       or      a2, a2, a3
-       slli    a4, a4, 32
-       slli    a5, a5, 48
-       or      a4, a4, a5
-       or      a2, a2, a4
-.Ltmp634:
+       vle16.v v8, (s0)
+       .loc    1 256 10 is_stmt 0              # MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.
c:256:10 @[ MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.c:441:5 @[ MultiSource/Benchmarks/nben
ch/emfloat.c:482:9 ] ]
+       vredor.vs       v8, v8, v8
+       vmv.x.s a2, v8
+.Ltmp639:
        #DEBUG_VALUE: IsMantissaZero:i <- 4
        .loc    1 441 5 is_stmt 1               # MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.
c:441:5 @[ MultiSource/Benchmarks/nbench/emfloat.c:482:9 ]
+       slli    a2, a2, 48
        beqz    a2, .LBB4_5
```

... I double-checked my before and after, and it seems you inverted them on your end?

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
I just double checked my diff and recreated it from scratch, it's not inverted unfortunately. I'm comparing f02aadc9456c05b17aaf2cef975212bacfe15357 (before) and 279b3dbec65c8bc898e254f373b4f443a7e106b9 (after), with the flags `-march=rva23u64 -O3` 

I also checked #182833 and it doesn't seem to fix the regression either.

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
I see the issue now, InstCombine scalarizes the vector load from <4 x i16> to i64, even though the alignment is 2:

```
attributes #0 = { "target-features"="+v" }
; *** IR Dump After PromotePass on after ***
define i1 @after(ptr %p) local_unnamed_addr #0 {
  %v = load <4 x i16>, ptr %p, align 2
  %cast = bitcast <4 x i16> %v to i64
  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %cast, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
; *** IR Dump After InstCombinePass on after ***
define i1 @after(ptr %p) local_unnamed_addr #0 {
  %v1 = load i64, ptr %p, align 2
  %cmp = icmp ne i64 %v1, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
```

My guess is that instcombine sees both loads as "unaligned" so it decides to scalarize it. But in fact the <4 x i16> is aligned, we just don't have a way of expressing this in the datalayout string yet.

https://discourse.llvm.org/t/rfc-add-a-data-layout-specifier-for-element-aligned-vectors/89764 is relevant here

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body: Hm, interesting. Looks like the patch exposed yet another opportunity for this sub-optimal fold?

comments.author: topperc
comments.body:
> My guess is that instcombine sees both loads as "unaligned" so it decides to scalarize it. But in fact the <4 x i16> is aligned, we just don't have a way of expressing this in the datalayout string yet.

It's probably done by `combineLoadToOperationType` in InstCombineLoadStoreAlloca.cpp. I don't think that function considers alignment.
